headline: Pesticide Poisoning Hospitalizes 4,800 Agricultural Workers Annually
context: Farm workers face high rates of pesticide exposure with 4,800 requiring hospital treatment in 2025.

cards.big: 4,800 agricultural workers hospitalized for pesticide poisoning in Malaysia during 2025
cards.sub: Actual exposure cases estimated at 10 times the hospitalization figure

cards.big: Malaysia still permits 35 pesticides banned in the EU including paraquat alternatives
cards.sub: Regulatory lag exposes workers and consumers to chemicals prohibited elsewhere

cards.big: Only 12% of plantation workers receive mandatory pesticide safety training per DOA surveys
cards.sub: Enforcement of Pesticide Act 1974 training requirements is negligible

cards.big: Sri Lanka banned paraquat in 2014 reducing agricultural poisoning deaths by 50%
cards.sub: Chemical bans proved more effective than training in preventing poisoning incidents

cards.big: Profits from banned chemicals come at the cost of workers' health and lives
Should Malaysia ban all pesticides that are prohibited in the EU within 3 years
